rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Dale Duncan And Marta Munoz Mendoza, 3rd Party Judgment Creditors Of Defendants, For Order Directing Receiver To List And Market Receivership Property For Sale (Hill Street)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2022 line 1. THIRD PARTY DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MENDOZA Notice Of Motion And Motion By Dale Duncan And Marta Munoz Mendoza, 3rd Party Judgment Creditors Of Defendants, For Order Directing Receiver To List And Market Receivership Property For Sale (Hill Street) is CONTINUED to May 9, 2022. The Court requires further evidence regarding the agreement between the judgment creditors and other lien holders. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSITY PRESIDENT ASSOCIATES, LP VS. RONNI OKON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF UNIVERSITY PRESIDENT ASSOCIATES, LP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff as moving party fails to provide admissible and undisputed evidence for the essential element of Plaintiff's performance in the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant causes of action. See CACI Instructions No. 303 and 325. There is also a triable issue of material fact for the essential element of harm/damages in all three causes of action as to whether the attorney fees in the TRO cases, Tom Bane case, and small claims/rent board cases are reasonable. See CACI Instructions No. 303, 325 and 2021. Plaintiff fails to shift its burden. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
